Manual Pre-Create Worktree Pattern in /ork:cover

PR orchestkit#1840 · Closes Yonatan-HQ/platform#3224 Path B · Generated 2026-05-16

The bug: Agent(isolation="worktree") in CC Opus 4.7 (Nov 2026 — May 2026) does NOT reliably isolate. Multiple parallel agents thrash the primary worktree's HEAD via sequential git checkout, cutting off at ~60 tool uses each.
The fix: Pre-create one worktree per agent in the lead. Pass the absolute path into each agent's prompt. Each agent's prompt's first Bash command is cd <wt-path>. Empirical: 4–22 tool-uses per agent vs 60–86 broken, zero cutoffs.

Before / After

# BROKEN: relies on isolation= which doesn't actually isolate in CC Opus 4.7
if "unit" in TIERS:
    Agent(
        subagent_type="test-generator",
        prompt=f"""Generate unit tests for: {SCOPE}
        ...""",
        isolation="worktree",           # ← thrashes HEAD when paired with parallel agents
        run_in_background=True,
        max_turns=50,
    )

Observed 2026-05-11: 4 parallel agents with isolation="worktree" → 3 of 4 operated in the PRIMARY worktree, each got cut off at 60–86 tool-uses pre-push.

# FIXED: lead pre-creates worktrees, agent prompts start with `cd <wt-path>`
import subprocess
WORKTREES = {
    "unit": "../platform-cover-unit-{slug}",
    "integration": "../platform-cover-int-{slug}",
    "e2e": "../platform-cover-e2e-{slug}",
}
for tier, wt_path in WORKTREES.items():
    if tier not in TIERS:
        continue
    subprocess.run([
        "git", "worktree", "add", "-b", f"cover/{slug}-{tier}",
        wt_path.format(slug=SCOPE_SLUG), "origin/main"
    ], check=True)

if "unit" in TIERS:
    Agent(
        subagent_type="test-generator",
        prompt=f"""FIRST: `cd {WORKTREES["unit"].format(slug=SCOPE_SLUG)}`
        — run this as the FIRST segment of EVERY Bash call. Stay in this
        worktree for the entire task. Do not switch branches.

        Generate unit tests for: {SCOPE}
        ...
        Push early: as soon as you have >=1 passing test, commit + push.
        Do NOT use --no-verify on commits.""",
        # isolation="worktree",  ← REMOVED — pre-created above
        run_in_background=True,
        max_turns=50,
    )

Empirical from M164 Wave 2/3: 4–22 tool-uses per agent, all complete in one run, zero cutoffs.

Why /ork:cover specifically

/ork:implement already had this warning + manual pattern since the M164 codification (see implement/SKILL.md Phase 5). /ork:cover was missing the warning and actively propagating the broken isolation="worktree" in its Phase 3 example. Operators following the literal example fell into the footgun. This PR closes that gap.

Definition of Done (per #3224)

CriterionOwnerThis PR
/ork:implement docs updated with the patterncode✓ already shipped (M164)
/ork:cover docs updated with the patterncode✓ this PR
Sub-agent invocations climb to >=3/session over 7doperator-side metricaccumulates naturally; track via cc-event SubagentStop hook
Zero "agent ran in wrong worktree" incidents in .claude/escape-hatches.jsonloperator-side metricaccumulates naturally

Diff summary

plugins/ork/skills/cover/SKILL.md
  + ~30 lines (warning block + Phase 3 example replacement)
  - 6 lines (old isolation="worktree" references)

plugins/ork/skills/cover/references/manual-worktree-pattern.md (NEW)
  + 141 lines (copy of /ork:implement's canonical doc)

Self-dogfood note

This PR's author session dogfooded the manual pre-create pattern multiple times today across 13 platform PRs, including parallel agent spawns for /ork:cover (issue #3484), /ork:review-pr (3 parallel reviewers), and /ork:assess (4 parallel architects). All used pre-created worktrees with cd <path> as first Bash. Zero cutoffs.
